PURPOSE OF NEUROFEEDBACK: It is to make the individual conscious by monitoring the normal and abnormal bioelectrical responses in the brain, which the individual does not consciously understand and realize, with the help of a tool, and to give the individual the ability to control his cognitive and behavioral physiology.

Neurobiofeedback Laboratory Applications

With biofeedback, individuals learn to control their bodily reactions that cause them distress. With electrodes attached to the knuckles of the fingers, muscle resistance, skin temperature, heart rate are monitored, and work is done to recognize the right and regulate the wrong one. With respiratory biofeedback, they learn to breathe correctly with the belt attached to the diaphragm, incorrect breathing is corrected, EMG biofeedback is used to relax the tense areas in the muscles and relaxation is taught with images and data. Vaginismus is solved with the apparatus used for vaginismus.

Feedback is called biofeedback if it is physiologically specific such as pulse, skin temperature, muscle resistance, neurofeedback if it is related to brain bioelectrical activities, and neurobiofeedback when both feedbacks can be applied together.

Techniques Applied in Neurobiofeedback Laboratory

Technically, Neurofeedback is basically an EEG system. Functionally, it is intended for regional "training". A region is identified in the QEEG that shows different bioelectric activity than normal. Electrodes are attached to this area. With the help of a pod system, bioelectric activation in the detected region is monitored. Computer controlled bioelectric data recordings are automatically filtered. This recorded activity is stored and analyzed. As a result, graphs are obtained that reflect the bioelectric activity in that session and strengthen the comparative performance evaluation and visual feedback between sessions

.

Regulatory studies are carried out

in Neurobiofeedback Laboratory

.

Neurobiofeedback is a system that is used to autogenically relax the brain, which is tired in today's fast-paced and stressor-rich daily life, and to strengthen the ability to avoid stressors and learn and use coping mechanisms. It is a system that can be used to protect against mental disorders that may arise due to stress and stress-based causes and to strengthen coping mechanisms.

Biofeedback is a technique that helps the person to control these symptoms by increasing their awareness of the physical symptoms of stress and, in a sense, to learn to relax and relax psychologically. In "Neuro-biofeedback" (neuro-body feedback), one of the most effective methods developed for this purpose, brain waves can be observed in a computerized environment in addition to the feedback of bodily reactions and the person can use them as a feedback tool. With this technique, which is applied within the psychotherapy process, people gain the ability to control stress. Stress This technique is important as a drug-free method to help individuals gain the ability to remain calm in a stressful environment. This practice, which means technological intervention in thought, provides the individual with an internal disciplinary structure that has a very positive effect on the creation of goal orientation and learning strategies and on ensuring the principles of continuity and discipline in the process of applying them.

Neurofeedback training teaches the person what the situation is like at the moment of specific brain waves and provides training on how to transition to these states through the use of willpower. The person receiving the training is able to control their brain activity according to the reactions required by the current situation and maintain situational physiological balance. Neurofeedback makes the brain more flexible and has an effect on the entire nervous system. What this means is quite impressive. Training the brain to eliminate its own dysregulation will not only affect the nervous system, but will also have a positive effect on the cardiovascular, digestive, hormonal and immune systems.
